LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT ORDERS CHARLTON H. BONHAM, DIRECTOR, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE; MICHAEL P. CARION, CHIEF, LAW ENFORCEMENT DIVISION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE, THEIR AGENTS, SERVANTS, ASSIGNS AND ALL THOSE ACTING IN CONCERT WITH THEM ARE RESTRAINED AND ENJOINED PENDING THE EARLIER OF TRIAL IN THIS ACTION, AND/OR JULY 1, 2013, FROM ISSUING CITATIONS TO RESTAURANTS FOR THE POSSESSION, SALE, OFFER FOR SALE, TRADE, OR DISTRIBUTION OF SHARK FINS- OR SHARK FINS PRODUCTS MADE FROM FINS- THAT WERE POSSESSED BY THAT RESTAURANT PRIOR TO JANUARY 1, 2012 AND ENJOINING DEFENDANTS FROM CONFISCATING THESE FINS. PLAINTIFF'S OTHER REQUESTED RELIEF IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOSEPH BREALL, ESQ., AND JILL DIAMOND, ESQ., 415.345.0545, FOR PLAINTIFF CHINATOWN NEIGHBORHOOD ASSOCIATION.
KARI KROGSENG, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 916.322.1067, FOR DEFENDANTS CHARLTON H. BONHAM, DIRECTOR, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE AND MICHAEL P. CARION, CHIEF, LAW ENFORCEMENT DIVISION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE.

RULING: Argued and the Court RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT ORDERS CHARLTON H. BONHAM, DIRECTOR, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE; MICHAEL P. CARION, CHIEF, LAW ENFORCEMENT DIVISION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE, THEIR AGENTS, SERVANTS, ASSIGNS AND ALL THOSE ACTING IN CONCERT WITH THEM ARE RESTRAINED AND ENJOINED PENDING THE EARLIER OF TRIAL IN THIS ACTION, AND/OR JULY 1, 2013, FROM ISSUING CITATIONS TO RESTAURANTS FOR THE POSSESSION, SALE, OFFER FOR SALE, TRADE, OR DISTRIBUTION OF SHARK FINS- OR SHARK FINS PRODUCTS MADE FROM FINS- THAT WERE POSSESSED BY THAT RESTAURANT PRIOR TO JANUARY 1, 2012 AND ENJOINING DEFENDANTS FROM CONFISCATING THESE FINS. PLAINTIFF'S OTHER REQUESTED RELIEF IS DENIED. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
